rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into component implementations only when the contract involves visible features or constants from the target scope.
Place assume/guarantee clauses inside annex agree only if they reference named constants, visible features, or concrete bounds from the target component.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture scope when inserting annex blocks; ensure all referenced identifiers are visible in the target context.
Avoid inserting AGREE clauses that reference AADL properties like Compute_Execution_Time or unit tokens unless they are explicitly exposed as typed ports or constants.
Use `assign` and `eq` statements to define relationships between visible features and contract expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, and `const` syntax to define contract expressions.
Conditional expressions in AGREE should use `if-then-else` to define feature relationships.
anti_patterns: Do not insert AGREE annex blocks that reference AADL property names as variables unless they are explicitly typed and visible.
Avoid placing assume/guarantee clauses in annex agree without ensuring all identifiers are visible in the target component.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE contract clause generation from requirements
